ShortDescription.Value: Make jaywalking expensive so pedestrians funnel to marked zebra crossings instead of cutting across roads anywhere.

Publish.LongDescription:
Pedestrians in Cities: Skylines II will happily cut straight across a road wherever it is shortest. This mod raises the pathfinding cost of crossing away from a marked crosswalk, so they strongly prefer to walk to a real zebra crossing — without ever getting stranded.
- The game already treats an unmarked crossing as "unsafe" (a mild ~100 cost vs ~5 for a zebra); it is a nudge, not a deterrent, so cims still jaywalk. This mod multiplies that unsafe cost by a factor you choose, turning the nudge into a real deterrent. Marked zebra crossings are left untouched and stay cheap, so pedestrians funnel to them.
- SAFE: the pedestrian pathfinder never fails on cost, so a pedestrian with no reachable marked crossing still crosses (just very reluctantly) — no one is ever stuck at a curb.
Options: - Jaywalk cost multiplier (1-30, default 10) — how hard pedestrians avoid unmarked crossings. - Re-apply interval (in-game hours) — the game can reset the cost after big road edits, so the mod periodically re-asserts it.
Everything is opt-in; turn the mod off for exact vanilla behaviour.
Source (open source, MIT): https://github.com/AmicusDeus/StopJaywalking
Made with Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool.

ChangeLog.Value: Fixed: your jaywalk-cost multiplier now re-applies instantly when you load a save. Previously, loading a save reset the crossing cost to vanilla and the mod waited until its next re-apply interval to restore it, so pedestrians briefly jaywalked freely right after a load. (Settings themselves persist as normal across sessions.)
